topic: How do you onboard a new PRO officer without losing track of open cases mid-handover?
format: Build a sharp before/after: managing government services with WhatsApp and Excel versus SmartPRO Hub case tracking and a client portal — show the same week, completely different experience
post:
Monday, 8:00 AM. New PRO officer starts today.

By 10:30 AM, three clients are calling about cases the old officer was handling. Nobody knows where the files are. The handover "notes" are a WhatsApp thread with 340 messages.

That's not a staffing problem. That's a systems problem.

Sanad offices running on WhatsApp and Excel don't have a case management system — they have a memory system. And memory walks out the door with every officer change.

Same Monday, on SmartPRO Hub:

New officer opens the platform. Every client company is there. Every open case — work permits, visa renewals, MOL filings — has a status, a deadline, and the last action logged. Nothing lives in someone's head. Nothing disappears.

Handover goes from three days of chaos to 20 minutes of orientation.

Once you're past 15 clients, this isn't a nice-to-have. Work permit tracking in Oman can't depend on which officer happens to remember what. I've seen what happens when it does — and it's always the client who pays for it.

The question isn't whether to fix this. It's how many more Monday mornings you can absorb before you do.

Try the Sanad AI Assistant free — instant answers on work permits, visas & government fees: https://www.thesmartpro.io/sanad/assistant?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=sanad-pro
